warnings: This is the largest/most complex beam plan encountered so far in this project (house #5, 224 sqm) -- many interior beam segments (B1/BX/B5/CN cluster around grid2-grid3) are closely spaced and only moderately legible at this image resolution; recorded with best-effort grid_ref positions and confidence 0.55-0.7, flagged for human review rather than guessed with false precision.

POSSIBLE DUMMY GRID: a beam (B1, dashed) extends from grid1-gridA southward, and a further beam (B2, dashed) runs grid1-grid2 at a level 2.05m south of grid A (per the left-side dimension chain: ...B(1.80)-A(0.0)-[2.05 further south]) -- this looks like a real structural extension (possibly a covered porch/carport slab, matching the 'ดูขยาย2' expansion-detail callout referenced twice in this area), not just a roof overhang. Tentatively named 'gridAprime' at pos_m=-2.05 relative to grid A; NOT yet added to the grid master file pending confirmation from S-05 (floor 2 beam plan) or S-07 (roof plan) showing the same feature independently.

A dashed linestyle is used for several beams in the southern half of the plan (B2 at grid A, B1/B2 in the extension zone) -- dashed usually means 'beam below current floor / not visible at this cut level' in these drawing sets, but this hasn't been cross-confirmed against the legend on S-01; recorded as printed.

The 0.60m/0.50m overhangs beyond grid1/grid4 at the top dimension chain (gridF row) appear to be eave/slab-edge dimensions rather than a beam-bearing dummy grid, since no beam is drawn beyond those points at the F line itself -- unlike the southern extension which does show real beams. Flagged as a lower-priority discrepancy vs. the southern extension.
